annotation.personal_flag: Desert Point reports 78% clean-wave probability in May — the dry season just started, swell is building, lineups at Selong Belanak are still sparse before the July influx.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 18–25 in Lombok: early dry season, 25–30°C, sea 27°C, SE trade winds beginning to establish, minimal rainfall. Surf quality 4/5: Desert Point has 78% clean day probability in May per surf-forecast.com, with S/SW groundswell building and offshore ENE winds. Selong Belanak solid for mid-level surfers, consistent and uncrowded. Crowd level low — dry season has started but the main Jul–Aug tourist surge has not arrived; guesthouses in south Lombok not yet at capacity and prices not yet peak. No local festivals or events in Lombok for this window (Yadnya Kasada is Java-based, no Lombok impact). Isolation score 4/5: southern Lombok (Desert Point, Selong Belanak) is genuinely off-the-beaten-path. No distortion factors — this is structural low-season pricing for a high-quality surf window.

notable_events: Yadnya Kasada festival on Mount Bromo / Java (no impact on Lombok)
annotation.personal_flag: June 19–29 is peak Desert Point season — consistent S/SW groundswell, offshore trade winds every morning; 10 nights to wait for the barrel of the trip.
annotation.raw_reasoning: June 19–29 in Lombok: peak dry season, 27°C daily, sea 27–28°C, reliable SE trade winds offshore all morning. Weather 5/5. Surf quality 5/5: June is when Desert Point and Selong Belanak fire at their best — S/SW groundswell at its most consistent, trade winds grooming conditions daily. The 10-night window maximises chances of catching Desert Point on a firing tide. Crowd level medium: Gili Islands fill up in June but south Lombok surf spots remain manageable; pre-July so not yet peak-peak. Yadnya Kasada is Java-based, no Lombok impact. Isolation score 3/5: slightly more visitors than May but southern Lombok retains its character. No distortion — annual seasonal pattern. €880 round trip for peak surf window is reasonable.

notable_events: Madeira Flower Festival (Apr 30–May 24) — Grand Parade May 22 in Funchal
Funchal Garden Festival — themed gardens across parks and promenades throughout May
annotation.personal_flag: The Flower Festival Grand Parade lands May 22 — the city peaks with bougainvillea floats and floral carpets during the exact midpoint of this stay.
annotation.raw_reasoning: May 17–23 in Funchal: 18–22°C highs, minimal rainfall, ~7h sunshine daily, sea 19°C. Classic Madeiran spring — comfortable for levada hiking and coastal walks. The Madeira Flower Festival runs Apr 30–May 24; the Grand Parade is May 22, falling squarely in the stay. Garden Festival also active. Crowds are medium — festival draws visitors but significantly below summer levels. Both events are annual recurring fixtures, not one-off anomalies, so no price distortion. Flight is essentially direct from MAD (2.67h outbound), return via LIS — excellent value at €190 round trip. Isolation score 2/5: Funchal is a city, but north coast and Fanal forest offer real seclusion on day trips. No surf relevance (hiking/ocean/mild-weather tags).

notable_events: Atlantic Festival (all June) — International Fireworks Competition, fireworks every Saturday night at 22:30
São João / Santos Populares (Jun 23) — bonfires, sardines, street parties across Funchal
São Pedro (Jun 29) — harbour celebrations, falls within stay
Summer Sunsets and Regional Arts Week (ongoing throughout June)
annotation.personal_flag: São João bonfires on Jun 23 and Atlantic Festival fireworks on Jun 27 both land in this stay — two of Madeira's biggest annual nights within 4 days of each other.
annotation.raw_reasoning: June 22–30 in Funchal: 22–24°C highs, ideal outdoor weather, very low rainfall, warm enough for swimming and hiking all day. Peak cultural month: Atlantic Festival runs all June with synchronized pyromusical fireworks every Saturday (Jun 27 falls within stay); Santos Populares São João is Jun 23, São Pedro Jun 29 — both within the 8-night window. Crowds are high, particularly on festival nights around the harbour; accommodation demand elevated. All events are annual recurring, no distortion flag. Price of €162 round trip is outstanding for an 8-night Madeira stay — notably cheaper than the May option despite more activity. Isolation score 2/5: Funchal city, festival-mode. North coast and Fanal accessible but Funchal itself will be busy. No surf relevance.

notable_events: Rainy season onset — monsoon brings heavy rainfall averaging 313mm in June
Rough seas likely — island-hopping tours at El Nido and Coron may be restricted or cancelled
annotation.personal_flag: June is rainy season in Palawan — 15–22 rain days expected and rough seas that routinely close the El Nido lagoon and Coron island-hopping tours central to the wishlist intent.
annotation.raw_reasoning: June 19–29 in Palawan (El Nido/Coron): wet season has arrived. Average 313mm rainfall in June, 15–22 rainy days, frequent afternoon thunderstorms, rough seas. Weather score 2/5. This directly conflicts with the island-hopping and maritime intent tags in the wishlist — lagoon access at El Nido and Coron island tours are regularly suspended during monsoon swells. Crowd level is low (off-season discount pricing, fewer tourists), and isolation score 4/5 (genuinely remote). However, the core activities the destination is listed for (island-hopping, snorkelling, clear-water maritime experiences) are severely weather-compromised. Wishlist note says 'avoid Mar–Apr (hottest)' but June wet season is a more fundamental problem for the itinerary. No distortion — this is structural monsoon-season pricing. At €1,147 it's the most expensive option and the least suitable season for the intent.
